content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

December 29, 1994

Mark W. Gilbert
Chief Legal Counsel
Sacramento Regional Transit District
Post Office Box 2110
Sacramento, California 95812-2110

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-94-363

Dear Mr. Gilbert:

As the Chief Legal Counsel for the Sacramento Regional
Transit District, you have requested advice regarding Government
Code Section 82048.5 of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1

QUESTIONS

1) Is the Sacramento Regional Transit District a special district
under Government Code Section 82048.5?

2) If so, is the Sacramento Regional Transit District subject to
the disclosure requirements of Government Code Section 53065.5?

CONCLUSIONS

1) The Sacramento Regional Transit District is a special
district.

2) Government Code Section 53065.5 is not under the jurisdiction
of the Act. Please contact the Attorney General's office for
advice regarding compliance with this section.

FACTS

Sacramento Regional Transit District ("RT") was formed
pursuant to Public Utilities Code Sections 101000 et seq.

1 Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory references
are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated. Commission
regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations, Sections
18000-18954. All references to regulations are to Title 2,
Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

File No. A-94-363
Page 2


ANALYSIS

Government Code Section 82048.5, effective January 1, 1995,
defines a "special district" as:

. . . any agency of the state established for the local
performance of governmental or proprietary functions
within limited boundaries. "Special district" includes
a county service area, a maintenance district or area,
an improvement district or zoning, an air pollution
control district, or a redevelopment agency. "Special
district" shall not include a city, county, city and
county, or school district.

The Sacramento Regional Transit District is an agency
established under the Public Utilities Code Section for the local
performance of governmental functions within Sacramento. Since it
is not specifically excluded from the definition of a special
district, as defined above, RT is considered a special district
under the Act.

The disclosure requirements of Government Code Section
53065.5 are not under the jurisdiction of the Act. Please contact
the Attorney General's office for advice regarding this section.

I trust this answers your questions.

Sincerely,


Steven G. Churchwell
General Counsel

By: Jim Stacher
    Counsel, Legal Division
